name: Firestore

unions.name: Error
unions.comment:
An error type

This type is available in order to disregard type of errors between protocol related errors as Http\_ or backend related errors as Response.

aliases.name: Document
aliases.comment: A record structure for a document fetched from Firestore.
aliases.type: { name : String.String, fields : a, createTime : Time.Posix, updateTime : Time.Posix }

aliases.name: FirestoreError
aliases.comment: Data structure for errors from Firestore
aliases.type: { code : Basics.Int, message : String.String, status : String.String }

aliases.name: ListOption
aliases.comment: Data structure for query parameter in calling `list` operation.

values.name: begin
values.comment: Starts a new transaction.

values.name: commit
values.comment:
Commits a transaction, while optionally updating documents.

Only `readWrite` transaction is currently supported which requires authorization that can be set via `Config.withAuthorization` function.

values.name: create
values.comment: Creates a new document.
values.type: Firestore.Decode.Decoder a -> Firestore.Encode.Encoder -> Firestore.Firestore -> Task.Task Firestore.Error (Firestore.Document a)

values.name: delete
values.comment: Deletes a document.

values.name: get
values.comment: Gets a single document.
values.type: Firestore.Decode.Decoder a -> Firestore.Firestore -> Task.Task Firestore.Error (Firestore.Document a)

values.name: init
values.comment: Builds a new Firestore connection with Config

values.name: list
values.comment: Lists documents.
values.type: Firestore.ListOption -> Firestore.Decode.Decoder a -> Firestore.Firestore -> Task.Task Firestore.Error (List.List (Firestore.Document a))

values.name: patch
values.comment: Updates or inserts a document.
values.type: Firestore.Decode.Decoder a -> Firestore.Encode.Encoder -> Firestore.Firestore -> Task.Task Firestore.Error (Firestore.Document a)

values.name: update
values.comment:
Adds a new update into the transaction

    model.firestore
        |> Firestore.commit
            (transaction
                |> Firestore.update draft1
                |> Firestore.update draft2
                |> Firestore.update draft3
            )
        |> Task.attempt Commited

values.name: withCollection
values.comment:
Drills down document paths with a specific path name.

This function is aimed to be chanind with pipeline operators in order to build up document path.

    firestore
        |> Firestore.collection "users"
        |> Firestore.collection "items"
        |> Firestore.collection "tags"
        |> Firestore.get
        |> Task.attempt GotUserItemTags

Of course, you can make it a single string as well

    firestore
        |> Firestore.collection "users/items/tags"
        |> Firestore.get
        |> Task.attempt GotUserItemTags

values.name: withConfig
values.comment: Updates configuration

name: Firestore.Config

unions.name: Config
unions.comment:
Data type for Firestore configuration

This type internally has all information which is required to send requests to Firestore through REST API.

values.name: endpoint
values.comment: Builds an endpoint string without path

values.name: httpHeader
values.comment: Extracts authorization with bearer prefix as `Http.Header`.

values.name: new
values.comment: Creates a new Config

values.name: withAuthorization
values.comment:
Specifies Firebase Authorization token which can be obtained through [`firebase.User#getIdToken`][verify_token] method.

[verify_token]: https://firebase.google.com/docs/auth/admin/verify-id-tokens#web

values.name: withDatabase
values.comment: Specifies database ID to connect to.

name: Firestore.Decode

unions.name: Decoder
unions.comment:
A decoder consisted of Firestore specific decoders.

`Json.Decode.Decoder` can be generate from this through `decode` function.

unions.name: Field
unions.comment: An identifier type for Firestore encoder

values.name: decode
values.comment: Generates Json.Decode.Decoder

values.name: document
values.comment:
Creates a new decoder

This function works like `Json.Decode.Pipeline`.

    Firestore.Decode.document Document
        |> Firestore.Decode.required "name" Firestore.Decode.string
        |> Firestore.Decode.required "age" Firestore.Decode.int
        |> Firestore.Decode.optional "canCode" Firestore.Decode.bool False

values.name: optional
values.comment:
Decodes an optional field.

This function is internally delegated into [`Json.Decode.Pipeline.optional`][optional].

[optional]: https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/NoRedInk/elm-json-decode-pipeline/latest/Json-Decode-Pipeline#optional
values.type: String.String -> Firestore.Decode.Field a -> a -> Firestore.Decode.Decoder (a -> b) -> Firestore.Decode.Decoder b

values.name: required
values.comment:
Decodes a required field.

This function is internally delegated into [`json.Decode.Pipeline.required`][required].

[required]: https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/NoRedInk/elm-json-decode-pipeline/latest/Json-Decode-Pipeline#required
values.type: String.String -> Firestore.Decode.Field a -> Firestore.Decode.Decoder (a -> b) -> Firestore.Decode.Decoder b

name: Firestore.Encode

unions.name: Encoder
unions.comment:
An encoder consisted of Firestore specific encoders.

This can be encoded into `Json.Value` through `encode` function.

unions.name: Field
unions.comment: An identifier type for Firestore encoder

values.name: document
values.comment:
Creates a new encoder

This function works like `Encode.object` but accepts a list of tuples which has only encoders provided from `Firestore.Encode` module

    Firestore.Encode.document
        [ ( "name", Firestore.Encode.string "IzumiSy" )
        , ( "age", Firestore.Encode.int 26 )
        , ( "canCode", Firestore.Encode.bool True )
        ]

values.name: encode
values.comment: Generates Json.Encode.Value from Encoder

name: Firestore.Types.Geopoint
comment:
Geopoint data type for Firestore

@docs Geopoint, new, latitude, longitude

name: Firestore.Types.Reference
comment:
Reference data type for Firestore

@docs Reference, new, toString
